candidates.why: Explicit D&A row exists, but it appears as a separate operating expense line alongside Personnel costs and Other operating expenses rather than as a COGS-specific subcomponent. No visible label ties it to cost of goods sold.
This is the main operating D&A-like summary row, but it sits outside the selected COGS row and is not labelled as COGS-related. It therefore does not look like COGS-specific D&A.

variables.notes: Selected COGS bucket is only 'Cost of goods sold'. The visible depreciation/amortisation rows appear as separate operating expense lines at the SG&A/overhead level, not as COGS-linked rows. To avoid misclassifying overhead D&A into COGS, no COGS_DA row is added.

candidates.why: This is the explicit same-level operating expense line outside the selected XSGA component rows ('Personnel costs' and 'Other operating expenses'), so it is the strongest evidence of D&A that should be added separately later. However, the label also includes impairment losses, which are outside scope for pure D&A.

This is a pure depreciation component beneath the broader D&A summary area and is in the candidate shortlist. It is relevant to SG&A-level D&A, but using it alone would undercapture amortisation and may reflect only one component of the broader summary.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation of Financial Lease Right-of-Use Assets
candidates.why: This appears to be another depreciation component under the broader D&A section and likely belongs with operating overhead rather than COGS. It should not be combined with the summary row to avoid double counting.
This likely contains amortisation relevant to SG&A-level D&A, but it is contaminated by impairment and appears to be a component under the broader summary block.

variables.notes: There is clearly a separate operating D&A block outside the selected XSGA component rows, so some XSGA_DA likely exists. But the best summary row includes impairment ('Depreciation, amortisation and impairment losses'), which the instructions say to ignore, while the component rows are partial and would either omit amortisation or risk contamination/double counting. Conservative output is empty pending manual review.
